items.mistake: Letting integration tests share state across runs.
items.fix: Fresh state per test (transactional rollback, drop-create, unique namespaces).
items.why: Shared state causes order-dependent flakiness — passes locally, fails in CI's parallel runner.
items.example:
```python
@pytest.fixture
def session(pg_engine):
    conn = pg_engine.connect(); tx = conn.begin()
    yield Session(bind=conn)
    tx.rollback(); conn.close()
```

items.why: Upstream releases new images regularly; CI flakes when a minor bump changes behaviour.

items.mistake: Running integration tests in the same suite as unit tests by default.
items.fix: Use markers (`@pytest.mark.integration`) and a default `-m 'not integration'` filter; opt in explicitly.
items.why: Mixing them slows local development; engineers stop running tests at all.

items.mistake: Hitting the real LLM provider on every PR.
items.fix: Use VCR cassettes for the per-PR run; a tiny `@pytest.mark.smoke` suite runs nightly with the real API.
items.why: Token spend, latency and provider outages turn PR CI into a coin flip.

items.mistake: Treating integration test flakes by adding `@pytest.mark.flaky(reruns=3)`.
items.fix: Investigate the root cause — shared state? real time? race condition? Fix it, don't paper over it.
items.why: Retrying flaky tests hides real bugs that will eventually break production.
items.example: A test that 'sometimes' fails after retry is telling you the system has a race condition. Find it.
